title: 20 Zlotys (Frédéric Chopin)

obverse.description: Golden (color), brown, black, tan and teal print; steel engraved portrait of Chopin in profile at center. Image of birthplace at Żelazowa Wola at left and score fragment of the Mazurka in B-Flat Major (Op. 7) on the right with facsimile of signature below.
obverse.lettering:
NARODOWY BANK POLSKI
DWADZIEŚCIA ZŁOTYCH
FRYDERYK CHOPIN
WARSZAWA, 19 MARCA 2009 r.
PREZES   GŁÓWNY SKARBNIK
obverse.lettering_translation:
National Bank of Poland
Twenty Zlotys
Frédéric Chopin
Warsaw, 19th March 2009
CEO  Main Treasurer

reverse.description: Red, golde (color), teal, brown and black print; red and black serial; stylized music stand at left center. Score fragment of Etude in F Minor (Op. 10, No. 9) over a landscape with willow trees.
reverse.lettering:
NARODOWY BANK POLSKI
BANKNOTY EMITOWANE PRZEZ NARODOWY BANK POLSKI SĄ PRAWNYM ŚRODKIEM PŁATNICZYM W POLSCE
DWADZIEŚCIA ZŁOTYCH
G. PFEIFER K. JARNUSZKIEWICZ DEL. P. KRAJEWSKI SC PWPW S.A.
NBP
reverse.lettering_translation:
National Bank of Poland
Banknotes issued by the National Bank of Poland are legal tender in Poland
Twenty Zlotys
G. PFEIFER K. JARNUSZKIEWICZ DEL. P. KRAJEWSKI SC PWPW S.A.
NBP

commemorated_topic: 200th Anniversary of the Birth of Frédéric Chopin
comments:
This note was issued in February and March 2010 and was sold solely at an internet auction by the Kolekcjoner service.
Visit the National Bank of Poland website to view the brochure on the note.

 

The note was issued in a folder, here is the front and back (the inside was blank, containing just the note in a plastic sleeve):

    

 

The note has a solid security strip which can be read under backlight. It has “20 ZŁ” repeated on it:

 

On the obverse the Chopin signature is in colour changing ink:

 

 

On the obverse at the lower left corner are two raised Xes:

 

On the reverse the sheet music design is in colour changing ink:

 

 

UV activity at 365nm. On the obverse the sheet music on the right has weak pale green fluorescence:

 

At 254nm this music design fluoresces strong red:

 

On the reverse only the left hand serial number fluoresces, in green. Here seen at 365nm:

© Brandon Bertolli
